intro: Echinospartum boissieri, commonly known as Boissier's spine-flower, is a shrub species in the Fabaceae family native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and inflammation, due to its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Echinospartum boissieri include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Echinospartum boissieri is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction to treat digestive issues, and is also used in the production of tinctures and capsules for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects.
